core_mechanism: Recovery of a latent state-dependent reweighting function (a Radon-Nikodym derivative, i.e. the density ratio between two probability measures) from a cross-section of observed expectation functionals of known payoff kernels, inverting the relation 'each observation = integral of a known kernel against the unknown density' to reconstruct the change of measure, then applying that reweighting to compute expectations under the target measure; the reweighting's shape is generated by a latent multiplicative (stochastic-volatility) diffusion factor.
brief_reason: The abstract recovers a stochastic discount factor, i.e. a reweighting function between the option-implied and physical distributions, purely from the cross-section of S&P 500 option prices, then uses it to compute a forward-looking expectation (the equity premium), with the recovered shape rationalized by stochastic-volatility dynamics under a constant market price of risk.
